summary: Tested new backup/restore feature on Resumen tab. UI/testIDs are correct and the section renders perfectly (cards visible: Exportar Ligero, Exportar Completo, Importar respaldo). Existing tabs (clientes/proveedores/reparto/resumen) all navigate fine and KPIs render. However the export flow is BROKEN ON WEB: tapping btn-export-light / btn-export-full does NOT show any Alert dialog because react-native-web does not implement Alert.alert (it is a no-op). As a result the user can never reach the confirmation step, exportBackup() is never invoked, and no JSON file is downloaded on web. The buttons themselves do not crash the app; they simply do nothing.

ui_bugs.issue: Tapping btn-export-light or btn-export-full does nothing on web. Alert.alert() is a no-op in react-native-web, so the 'Exportar respaldo' confirmation dialog never renders and the onPress confirm callback (which calls exportBackup) never fires. No download is triggered. Verified by checking document.body.innerText after tap: neither 'Exportar respaldo' nor 'Cancelar' appear in DOM, and only 2 'Exportar' texts exist (the two card labels), no Alert buttons. No browser download event was captured.

Same Alert.alert no-op issue affects the post-pick confirmation step of import on web (after a file is picked, the 'Confirmar restauración' dialog will not appear and replaceAll will never run). The button itself does not crash.

integration_issues.issue: Alert.alert confirm never appears -> exportBackup() never called -> no Blob/a.download executed. Web download flow effectively broken.

react_native_issues.issue: Alert.alert is unsupported on react-native-web. All flows that gate logic behind an Alert button (export confirm, import confirm, delete contact, delete reparto, date picker fallback errors) will silently fail on web.

action_items: Replace Alert.alert in ResumenView export/import flows with a Platform-aware confirmation. On web use window.confirm() OR a custom RN <Modal> with testIDs (e.g., btn-confirm-export). The custom Modal approach is preferred for testability and consistency with the existing modals already in index.tsx.
Consider doing the same for other Alert.alert usages (delete contact, delete reparto, photo permissions) so the web preview is fully functional.

After fix, verify on web: tapping btn-export-light shows a confirm dialog with a testID-tagged confirm button, clicking it triggers a browser download of gestion-contable-YYYY-MM-DD.json containing {app:'gestion-contable-pro', version:1, exportedAt, data:{clients,providers,repartos}} and no 'data:image' base64 strings.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx ResumenView.doExport and doImport rely on Alert.alert button callbacks which never fire on web. Wrap with Platform.OS==='web' ? window.confirm(...) : Alert.alert(...) or use a shared RN Modal component.

backup.ts is well structured and correct: stripPhotos removes photo base64, web branch uses Blob + a.download, native branch uses FileSystem + Sharing. Logic itself is fine; the only blocker is the gating Alert on web.
store.ts replaceAll action is implemented correctly and persists via update().

success_rate.frontend: 70% (UI renders & navigation OK; export/import gated by Alert on web is broken)

seed_data_creation: No seed data created; used the default seeded data already loaded by the store.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once main agent replaces Alert.alert with a web-compatible confirmation (window.confirm or RN Modal with testIDs like btn-confirm-export-light/btn-confirm-export-full/btn-confirm-import), re-test by: (1) tapping btn-export-light -> confirm -> capture page.on('download') event, parse the JSON, assert app=='gestion-contable-pro', version==1, no 'data:image' substring. (2) Same for btn-export-full but assert it MAY contain photos. (3) Tap btn-import-backup and verify it does not crash and opens a file input (input[type=file] should appear in DOM on web).
rca_of_issue: Root cause: react-native-web does not implement Alert.alert with buttons (it falls back to a no-op or simple window.alert without button callbacks). Reproduction: open https://ledger-grid-1.preview.emergentagent.com -> Resumen tab -> scroll to 'Respaldo de datos' -> tap 'Exportar (Ligero)'. Expected: confirmation dialog 'Exportar respaldo' with Cancelar/Exportar. Actual: nothing happens, no dialog text in DOM, no download. Mitigation: use Platform.select to call window.confirm on web, or build a custom RN <Modal> dialog (preferred for testIDs).
